profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner and main organizer of the household finances; tends to lead meetings and set the agenda.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (comfortable with account types, taxes, and investment terminology; tends to track balances and notices small discrepancies).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but protection-first: prefers to avoid big drawdowns, asks about downside scenarios, and is willing to accept market risk only if there’s a clear plan and guardrails.
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; often comes with a mental checklist. Uses specific numbers when she remembers them, but will occasionally pause with 'let me think' or 'I might be off by a bit' before correcting herself. Will politely interrupt to clarify terms or confirm whether figures are gross vs. net.
emotional_tendencies: gets uneasy when expenses feel high relative to cash on hand
relieved by clear summaries and action steps
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and comparative; wants to see options side-by-side and will push for a decision once she feels the tradeoffs are explicit.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open; trusts more when the advisor is precise, repeats back numbers accurately, and explains the 'why' without sales pressure.
internal_biases: loss aversion (overweights avoiding regret from a market drop)
anchoring to current cash balance as a comfort metric
hidden_concerns: worries they are 'behind' on long-term savings despite decent income
concerned about paying for protection and whether it’s the right amount/value

profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; contributes income and owns the home in his name, but tends to defer on investing details unless prompted.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (understands basics like budgeting and retirement accounts, but mixes up labels like premium vs. benefit or gross vs. net; prefers plain-English explanations).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a slight optimism tilt: comfortable with some volatility if it feels like 'long-term growth,' but can get anxious when conversations focus on worst-case outcomes or complex tax details.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and informal; speaks in round numbers and uses phrases like 'roughly' or 'around.' Will ask quick clarifying questions and sometimes answers before thinking, then backtracks with 'wait—actually' when corrected. Less likely to volunteer exact figures unless Mary cues him.
emotional_tendencies: prefers reassurance that the plan is simple and manageable
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants Mary to feel comfortable, then signs on once he understands the headline impact on monthly cash flow and lifestyle.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive; builds trust through rapport and clear next steps rather than technical depth.
internal_biases: present bias (focuses on monthly budget impact more than distant retirement)
optimism bias (assumes things will work out if they keep earning)
hidden_concerns: worries about being judged for not knowing all the financial terms
concerned about keeping flexibility in case job/income changes
